title: New Komm Susser Tod
wikitext:
[]: New 'Komm Susser Tod' Post by gwern » Sun Feb 19, 2012 9:54 pm I have been told by a certain person that a new version of 'Komm Susser Tod' is in the works. The obvious inference for why a song from 15 years ago would get a rework is that it will be used in Rebuild, which would be quite interesting. Does anyone know anything which might bear on this? Could there be an anniversary album collection scheduled or something?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 19, 2012 10:03 pm It'd be nice to see a new version of Komm Susser Tod during Final, especially if it's used in a different context. Though, two KST-esque songs have already appeared in 2.22; hopefully a break is taken during 3.0, so as to give the new KST all the more impact in Final, if it happens at all.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Sun Feb 19, 2012 10:18 pm It's not for the films. Apparently Arianne is just doing it herself, according to her Facebook. More discussion in this thread: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/post/521772/Komm-Susser-Tod-Who-is-Arianne/?highlight=#521772[/url]

[]: Post by gwern » Sun Feb 19, 2012 10:26 pm Ah, I guess that's it. Thanks!

[]: Post by Rocky Raccoon » Mon Feb 20, 2012 12:57 am Speaking of songs, why are they subtitled on the BD? It's fucking frustrating to all hell.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Mon Feb 20, 2012 6:33 am Not sure if this is on-topic, but I am hearing subtle nods to the original "Komm Süsser Tod" (KST) in the Rebuild soundtrack. Listen carefully to "The Red Ambassadrice" (which is the song that plays during Asuka's introductory battle at the start of 2.0). You will hear a slightly dissonant fragment of KST as a background melody. It sent chills down my spine the first time I noticed, because it seems out of place. KST played as Human Instrumentality was realized, but NGE/EoE-Asuka was presented as something of an antithesis to instrumentality. Could this be a clue that Rebuild-Asuka will be an agent of Human Instrumentality in the end?

[]: Post by liquidus118 » Mon Feb 20, 2012 12:33 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Not sure if this is on-topic, but I am hearing subtle nods to the original "Komm Süsser Tod" (KST) in the Rebuild soundtrack. Listen carefully to "The Red Ambassadrice" (which is the song that plays during Asuka's introductory battle at the start of 2.0). You will hear a slightly dissonant fragment of KST as a background melody. It sent chills down my spine the first time I noticed, because it seems out of place. KST played as Human Instrumentality was realized, but NGE/EoE-Asuka was presented as something of an antithesis to instrumentality. Could this be a clue that Rebuild-Asuka will be an agent of Human Instrumentality in the end?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 20, 2012 12:34 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Listen carefully to "The Red Ambassadrice" (which is the song that plays during Asuka's introductory battle at the start of 2.0). You will hear a slightly dissonant fragment of KST as a background melody.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Mon Feb 20, 2012 12:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I think I caught it but I'm not quite sure. Timestamp?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 20, 2012 1:02 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: the little background chord sounds exactly like part of the instrumental accompaniment to "Komm süsser Tod".

[]: Post by Azathoth » Mon Feb 20, 2012 1:06 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: I am no expert on music, but to my layman's ear, the little background chord sounds exactly like part of the instrumental accompaniment to "Komm süsser Tod".

[]: Post by chaosakita » Mon Feb 20, 2012 1:08 pm I can't wait to hear what the new version sounds like. Too bad Arianne hasn't provided any updates since her original post.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Mon Feb 20, 2012 1:32 pm Azathoth wrote: If you mean the bit I think you mean, you're reaching. That was part of NGE's Eva-02 theme as well, it being more or less identical to Red Ambassadress and all that.

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 20, 2012 1:36 pm It's just a plain major chord, as ordinary and common as the color blue. Not even a weird inversion, root, 3rd, 5th.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Feb 20, 2012 4:48 pm Here's the post where I first made that whacked out theory involving horrific use of Asuka. More here. And here's a documentation of several 'firsties' of mine that will no doubt all come true. re:topic: Wow this thread is all over the place... Arianne confirmed herself that the new KST version she's doing isn't for the movies, and as for the musical callback moment being speculated about, I'm afraid I don't hear it.

[]: Post by gwern » Mon Feb 20, 2012 5:17 pm OK, truth time: the person was someone claiming to be Arianne: http://www.gwern.net/komm-susser-tod#comment-443744763 hello...i am arianne the singer mentioned above...i sand kst and everything you've ever dreamed....i am in the middle of working on a new version of KST!! i hope you will like it.... i also have lots of my own original material

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Feb 20, 2012 5:21 pm gwern wrote: What's Arianne doing in Australia?

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Feb 20, 2012 5:22 pm gwern: The Arianne stuff is based on her Facebook profile and other stuff. I recommend reading this thread starting here before casting around too many wild accusations.

[]: Post by gwern » Mon Feb 20, 2012 5:40 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I don't know if it's really that weird, people can live wherever they want, for example I read that Yuko Miyamura is also living in Australia or am I wrong?

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Feb 20, 2012 5:45 pm gwern wrote: Which doesn't disprove the comment - the language and email were consistent, for example - but still worth asking.

The obvious inference for why a song from 15 years ago would get a rework is that it will be used in Rebuild, which would be quite interesting. Does anyone know anything which might bear on this? Could there be an anniversary album collection scheduled or something?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 19, 2012 10:03 pm It&#3
